title: "podman commit" should support --signature-policy flag
body:
### Feature request description

podman load and podman pull command all support --signature-policy flag， why podman commit not? I need it as some customer environment has     their own podman, I need to scp my podman binary file and configuration file, but I don't want to use customer's policy.json when I need to run "podman commit"

### Suggest potential solution

Add --signature-policy flag to podman commit command

### Have you considered any alternatives?

A clear and concise description of any alternative solutions or features you've considered.

### Additional context

Add any other context or screenshots about the feature request here.

comments.body:
@beautylim interested in opening a PR? I think this could help as a reference: https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/15870 

@Luap99 @mheon @mtrmac just checking if there's any blocker for `podman commit` to not support this flag.

`--signature-policy` is not a documented flag - it's marked hidden and not in the manpages. It's more for our own CI testing than internal use, AFAIK. So I don't know if we want to expand its use.

Putting that aside, I'm somewhat confused as to why `podman commit` needs the flag. The `--signature-policy` flag is usually for operations that import images into Podman. `podman commit` does create a new image, but it only does so based off an existing one - there's no registries, no external files involved. Why do you need `--signature-policy` in this case?

> Putting that aside, I'm somewhat confused as to why `podman commit` needs the flag.

Same. `buildah/Builder.Commit` _explicitly overrides_ `policy.json` to allow reads from the c/storage source, so how does a policy make a difference? I guess, unless the host’s policy were completely invalid and failed to parse.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
